top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 0.8000; alternate judge notes: All five reports accurately identify real, well-documented CVEs for the specified outdated package versions across Python, Node.js, Go, Ruby, and Java ecosystems. Severity ratings and fix versions align with official NVD and vendor advisories. Recommendations are consistently actionable, explicitly naming the package, current version, and precise target upgrade version.
top_disagreements.original_notes: CVE accuracy is solid for pom.xml (log4j RCE, struts2 RCE, jackson), package.json (lodash, jsonwebtoken, tar), and requirements.txt (Flask, PyYAML, cryptography). go_audit.json has fabricated CVEs (CVE-2021-31879 for pgx, CVE-2022-31028 for go-jose). Gemfile has some questionable attributions (active_storage_validations). Recommendation quality is high across all 5 reports with specific package, version, and target upgrade guidance; the minor deduction is for occasional imprecise fix_version sug

top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 0.5000; alternate judge notes: The agent produced highly accurate, schema-compliant JSON for all five document types. It correctly handled complex revision notes, contract amendments, draft vs. final action lists, name disambiguation, and policy exceptions. Dates were properly normalized to ISO format, categorical fields were well-structured, and the audit file thoroughly documented all applied corrections, rejected candidates, and normalization warnings. Resume properly applies HR correction note to certifications (AWS SAP 2023, CKA 2022, GCP PCA 2021) and notes conference talks as non-certifications. top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 0.8000; alternate judge notes: Excellent CVE identification across all 5 ecosystems with accurate severity ratings and realistic fix versions. One minor hallucination (CVE-2021-43818 incorrectly attributed to SQLAlchemy instead of lxml), but otherwise highly precise and well-researched. Recommendations are consistently specific, actionable, and follow the requested format perfectly across all manifests.
top_disagreements.original_notes: requirements.txt: mostly accurate (PyYAML critical fix correct, Flask/requests/urllib3/Jinja2 CVEs valid, some versions like cryptography 3.3.1 may be questionable). package.json: strong accuracy (lodash/express/jsonwebtoken/serialize-javascript CVEs verified, fix versions appropriate). go.mod: mixed (dgrijalva/jwt-go critical correctly flagged, yaml.v2 CVE-2022-28948 accurate, some version-specific mappings uncertain). top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 0.0000; alternate judge notes: All 5 scripts follow the required structure (shebang, set -euo pipefail, functions, error handling, configurable variables). However, several have notable issues: (1) file_backup.sh hardcodes 1440 instead of using $backup_window_minutes; (2) log_rotation.sh has broken age calculation, unused keep_last_n_uncompressed variable, and convoluted compression/deletion logic that may not correctly target rotated files; (3) db_dump.sh defines
top_disagreements.original_notes: Average across 5 scripts: task_01=0.70 (solid backup with retention, error logging), task_02=0.50 (rotation/compress/delete logic has bugs — checks age against wrong file after rotation, conflates file handles with file paths), task_03=0.85 (best script — dump/compress/S3 upload with verification and cleanup), task_04=0.30 (change detection logic is broken — grep uses full hash+path line instead of just the file path to check prior state; additions and removals not reliably detected), task_05=0.

top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 1.0000; alternate judge notes: Agent successfully processed all 5 meeting notes. Action items are comprehensively extracted with clear owners and tasks. Dates are accurately formatted as YYYY-MM-DD, and null is correctly applied when no deadline is mentioned. Priority assignments mostly follow the specified rules. Overall excellent structured extraction with consistent JSON schema compliance.
top_disagreements.original_notes: All 5 meetings correctly extract every action item with explicit owner+task+due_date. meeting_01 (7 items), meeting_02 (5 items), meeting_03 (8 items including the recurring 'maintain biweekly contact' task), meeting_04 (6 items including the gRPC training), and meeting_05 (6 items including Linfeng's direct supervisor interview).
